summary: Iteration 8 re-review of the Dashboard UI against guides/backend-guide.md and guides/ui-style-guide.md. Only input.json changed since iteration 7 (no code moved), so I re-read the full diff and re-verified every finding against the current tree (app/queries/dashboard/*, app/services/dashboard/broadcast.rb, app/controllers/home_controller.rb + phases_controller.rb, app/views/home/*, app/helpers/{status,define}_helper.rb, and the service broadcast wiring in Complete/Sweep/Approve/BroadcastCard/Column) rather than assuming. Architecture remains strongly guide-aligned: business logic lives in query-object POROs that materialize everything the view needs (nothing left lazy for the view); HomeController and PhasesController stay thin (delegate + branch only on result.success?/response format, one primary read per panel); broadcasts fire from services after the write commits and target the smallest DOM unit (dom_id(pipeline, :dashboard_row)) over a per-user :dashboard stream, with progress ticks opting out via dashboard:false so only state changes fan out; status_helper#status_badge is the single semantic-tone source and always shows the status word (color never alone); HomeController#safely wraps exactly one read per panel over read-only queries; Minitest covers queries, the broadcast service, controllers, and system flows. Both genuinely new patterns (presentation-boundary rescue; the cross-pipeline per-user stream) were added to the guides in the same PR exactly as CLAUDE.md requires. No blockers or majors. Six minor, guide-cited deviations remain unaddressed from prior iterations, each trivially fixable and none disturbing the sound architecture or changing behavior.

findings.issue: Off-scale semantic status colors. ui-style-guide.md > Color reserves `red-600` for stuck/failed/blocked and `green-600` for success/converged. The attention border (line 10) uses `border-red-500` and the completed-phase progress segments (line 29) use `bg-green-500`; the sibling `border-amber-500` and `bg-indigo-600` are on-scale, so only the red/green fills drift to the -500 step, and no other view uses solid -500 status fills. Fix: `border-red-600` / `bg-green-600`. Severity: minor.

Hand-rolled badge bypasses the single-source component. ui-style-guide.md > Core components requires 'one source of truth per component', and status_helper.rb#status_badge already renders the soft pill for both tones and accepts `label:`. The attention pill instead inlines the full class string (`bg-amber-50 text-amber-700 ring-1 ring-amber-600/20` and the red equivalent); status_badge("awaiting_human", label: "Needs your input") / status_badge("stuck", label: "Stuck") produce the same pill (the only extras being gap-1 and the decorative dot). The inline copy also omits the helper's `ring-inset` — precisely the drift a shared component prevents. Severity: minor.

Business logic in a view helper rather than a reusable PORO. backend-guide.md > 'Business logic lives in reusable POROs' says such logic belongs in plain Ruby objects callable from controllers, jobs, the console, and tests — not a helper mixed into the view layer. define_open_questions_structured / latest_structured_questions_run select the authoritative StepRun across phases->workflows->step_runs (flat_map + max_by on [iteration, attempt, id]) and JSON-parse the artifact payload, duplicating the near-identical run-selection in latest_open_questions_run. This selection/parse logic belongs in a query or domain object, with the helper reduced to a thin call. Severity: minor.

Empty states omit the icon and primary action the guide mandates. ui-style-guide.md > Core components: 'Empty states: icon + one sentence + primary action. Never a bare empty table.' 'No active pipelines', 'No recent activity', and 'No workers connected' provide only text (no icon, no action), while index.html.erb's 'No projects yet' state does include a primary action — an inconsistency within the same PR. The workers case can link to Workers/Projects even though worker registration is a CLI action. Severity: minor.

Fleet panel is kept current by fixed-interval client polling (turbo_frame_tag ... src: + poll_frame_controller.js reloading every 30s) rather than Turbo Stream broadcasts. ui-style-guide.md > Principles #3 ('Live by default … updates in place via Turbo Streams') and Real-time behavior push for stream-driven worker-state updates. This is a deliberate, documented deviation (worker heartbeats don't broadcast to the dashboard and per-heartbeat fan-out would storm), and it does avoid a manual refresh — but it departs from the stream-first convention and, unlike the two sanctioned new patterns, neither guide was amended to allow the poll fallback (CLAUDE.md: when the guide is silent, follow its principles and propose a guide addition in the same PR). Add a one-line guide note sanctioning the poll fallback, or record an explicit manager decision. Severity: minor.

Off-scale spacing values. ui-style-guide.md > Layout: 'Spacing scale: stick to Tailwind steps 2, 4, 6, 8, 12 … Avoid one-off values.' The dashboard partials use sub-scale steps outside that set: `mt-0.5` (pipeline_row:25, activity_item:4, fleet_health_content:36) and `py-1.5`/`h-1.5` (pipeline_row:29,46). The badge `py-0.5` is separately sanctioned by the StatusBadge spec, but these are not; the small button at pipeline_row:46 also uses `py-1.5` where the guide's button spec is `px-3 py-2`. Density is allowed where it earns it, but these are literal one-off values the rule names explicitly. Severity: minor.
